cand_a.notes: H3 falsified (68.4% < 75%); H5 preempted by ACAR; Yang et al. collision undermines H1-H2 novelty

cand_b.notes: Standard entropy routing, preempted by ACAR finding entropy ineffective

cand_c.notes: Genuinely novel (first error-depth-targeted training), GPU available via PyTorch 2.11.0, Li 2026 supports calculation error dominance

cand_d.notes: Safe fallback with limited impact; H1-H2 validation paper acknowledging Yang et al. collision

reasons: cand_a H3 falsified: Ea-based routing does NOT predict single-pass threshold (68.4% < 75%)
cand_a H5 HIGH RISK: ACAR 2026 explicitly found entropy routing shows weak correlation with ground truth
Yang et al. 2025 collision: Exponential saturation formula already published, H1-H2 are replications not novel
cand_c is genuinely novel: First error-depth-targeted training (EDTT) combining GRPO with depth-aware rewards
Li 2026 (Error Depth Hypothesis) supports cand_c: calculation errors 62% in low-depth failures
GPU now available: PyTorch 2.11.0 supports RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell (sm_120), enabling Round 2 CCAR-style training that previously failed
next_actions: Design EDTT pilot experiment: classify errors by depth (L1/L2/L3 per Li 2026 taxonomy)
Implement depth-aware GRPO rewards targeting L3 (hardest-to-correct) errors
Run pilot: EDTT vs standard GRPO on Qwen2.5-Math-7B
Validate H_C1: EDTT reduces L3 error rate more than standard GRPO
Validate H_C2: Error depth targeting improves over generic training by >5%

fallback_plan.if_edtt_pilot_fails: Pivot to cand_d (H1-H2 validation paper with H3 negative result)
fallback_plan.fallback_notes: Acknowledge Yang et al. collision explicitly; focus on empirical validation + per-level accuracy analysis on Qwen2.5-Math-7B

pivot_trigger.reason: cand_a H3 falsified; cand_a H5 preempted by ACAR concurrent work
pivot_trigger.evidence: Low-Ea problems: 68.4% accuracy (threshold >75%); ACAR 2026: entropy weak correlation
